title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nate DeMontigny found a pristine derringer pistol while magnet fishing in a Bridgewater river, encased like new.
The find, shared on March 12, gained over 1.2 million views online within two days.
DeMontigny contacted police about the pistol, which stood out for its preservation despite likely decades underwater.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sergio Josue Castellanos, 18, charged with murder in Chelsea stabbing incident.
A 25-year-old Chelsea man died in surgery from multiple stab wounds.
Chelsea police found multiple victims; investigation is ongoing.

title: Two officers injured in struggle with knife-wielding man at Forest Hills station, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Two officers were injured while subduing a knife-wielding man at Forest Hills MBTA station in Jamaica Plain.
The suspect attempted to stab bus tires and grabbed an officer's firearm, firing it once without hitting anyone.
The MBTA upper busway was closed for police activity, but regular service resumed two hours later.

title: Man accused of kicking dog in Roxbury before being assaulted by group
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A man in Roxbury faces animal cruelty charges for allegedly kicking a dog onto the street near 559 Dudley St.
The dog, named Biscuit, remains missing after the incident, prompting public appeals for its safe return.
The suspect was reportedly assaulted by five men following the incident; he sustained cuts and complained of dizziness.

title: Mass. man charged with threatening FBI director’s girlfriend
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Alden Welch Ruml of Cambridge charged with threatening email to girlfriend of FBI Director Kash Patel.
Email threatened assault on Alexis Wilkins due to news article about FBI security resources and political ties.
Ruml pleaded not guilty; faces up to five years if convicted, as per U.S. Attorney’s Office in Massachusetts.

title: Board a lobster boat and dine on your catch while staying at this Boston hotel
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Lenox in Boston offers the Boston Harbor Lobster Experience, featuring a lobster boat tour and dinner.
Guests catch their own lobster and enjoy a five-course dinner prepared by Chef Daniel Kenney at Willow & Ivy.
The package, including accommodations and transportation, starts in May with prices at $1,650 for two and $1,950 for four.

title: Red Line car shells cleared from customs, reviving replacement project
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Red Line train car shells detained by customs have been cleared, allowing manufacturing to restart at CRRC's Springfield factory.
The release of the shells is expected to bring back over 140 furloughed workers to the CRRC MA facility by mid-April.
The car shells were detained due to a U.S. Customs notice under the Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act, requiring documentation evidence.

title: He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to fight addiction stigma
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kevin Hayden, 47, from North Easton, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for addiction awareness.
He is participating with Learn to Cope, a nonprofit supporting families affected by addiction.
Hayden aims to challenge addiction stigma and honor loved ones lost to addiction.

title: Social media celeb Alix Earle returned to Harvard this week
author: John Waller
bullets: Alix Earle visited Harvard University to speak with Business School students and initiate a case study.
Earle addressed students in Professor Reza Satchu's class, known for hosting other celebrity guests.
She previously visited Harvard last spring, drawing comparisons to Reese Witherspoon's 'Legally Blonde' character.

title: ‘There’s nothing compared to that feeling’: Red Sox players discussed return from World Baseball Classic
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Red Sox players return to spring training after participating in the World Baseball Classic.
Venezuela wins its first World Baseball Classic, featuring Red Sox players Ranger Suarez, Wilyer Abreu, and Willson Contreras.
Opening Day for the Red Sox is March 26 in Cincinnati against the Reds at 4:10 p.m.
